steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: Both texts criticize scholars for fixating on the fragmentation-versus-coherence debate in modernist fiction at the expense of other stylistic elements. The correct answer must reflect that shared concern about this narrow focus distorting the picture of the period.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — It must widen its focus to include aspects of modernist fiction beyond style, a productive but over-represented site of inquiry.: goes further than either text argues — both stay within stylistic analysis. ✗
  • Choice B — Without a major shift in focus, the vision that it presents of fiction written in the period will continue to be unnecessarily limited.: directly captures both texts' warning that the fragmentation-versus-coherence fixation narrows understanding. ✓
  • Choice C — Its primary methods for analyzing fiction written in the period are growing obsolete as computer technology advances.: neither text calls the methods obsolete, only the singular focus. ✗
  • Choice D — Instead of engaging in unproductive debates, it should work to rehabilitate the reputations of neglected modernist works.: off-topic — neither text discusses individual authors' reputations. ✗
That is the correct answer.
